lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Kinston Regional Jetport – Piedmont operated LGA service until merger into USAir Aug 1989
Wikipedia: Kinston Regional Jetport – USAir mainline Fokker F28 service ended May 1992
Wikipedia: Kinston Regional Jetport – nonstop to BWI added 1984, USAir mainline ended May 1992
Wikipedia: Kinston Regional Jetport – Piedmont served Richmond until 1989 merger
Wikipedia: Kinston Regional Jetport – Piedmont served Wilmington NC until merger
Wikipedia: Kinston Regional Jetport – American Eagle served RDU May–Oct 1990

note: Delta Connection ATL service 2005–Jan 2007 and Allegiant SFB service Nov 2006–Apr 2008 also lost; existing record shows only ATL and CLT

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Rocky Mount–Wilson Regional Airport – US Airways Express suspended March 2001
Wikipedia: Rocky Mount–Wilson Regional Airport – Piedmont served RMT/RWI 1961–1980

detail: CCAir operating as US Airways Express suspended service in March 2001 after Southwest Airlines' 1999 entry at nearby RDU undercut demand; airport has had no scheduled service since.

lostDestinations.source: smithreynolds.org history – CCAir ceased Charlotte service January 2000
Search result: USAir/US Airways crew base closed INT 1991; mainline service wound down early 1990s
History of Smith Reynolds Airport – USAir served multiple hubs before paring to Charlotte only by 1999

detail: Commercial enplanements fell from 23,000 in 1990 to 7,000 in 1999 as service consolidated to Charlotte-only; last flight departed January 2000 when CCAir ended its US Airways Express code-share.

lostDestinations.source: aviationpros.com – Delta Connection CRJ200 ATL service lasted ~9 months, ended fall 2005
Wikipedia: Hickory Regional Airport – CCAir Sunbird operated up to 11 daily CLT flights through 2002
Wikipedia: Hickory Regional Airport – Atlantis Airlines 1979–1985 served Charlotte and Atlanta

detail: Delta Connection (Atlantic Southeast Airlines) using CRJ200s inaugurated ATL service in early 2005 but ceased after only nine months, leaving Hickory without any scheduled service.
source: Wikipedia: Hickory Regional Airport; aviationpros.com article on Delta's Hickory service

lostDestinations.source: moorecountyairport.com history – CCAir USAir Express served CLT Oct 1991–Apr 15 2002
moorecountyairport.com history – Delta Connection seasonal CRJ service 2006–Nov 2007

detail: CCAir/US Airways Express served Charlotte from October 1991 until April 15, 2002; Delta Connection returned briefly with seasonal ATL CRJ flights in 2006 ending November 2007, since which no scheduled service has operated.
source: moorecountyairport.com/history/; Wikipedia: Moore County Airport (North Carolina)

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Coastal Carolina Regional Airport – historical Delta/regional service to Atlanta ended mid-2000s
flyewn.com history – US Airways Express served multiple hubs including DCA through late 1990s

detail: New Bern retains American Airlines nonstop to Charlotte and Breeze Airways twice-weekly service to Hartford and Orlando as of 2024, but has lost former connections to Atlanta, Washington, and other hubs served by Piedmont/USAir since the 1980s.
source: flyewn.com/business/our-history/; Wikipedia: Coastal Carolina Regional Airport

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Fayetteville Regional Airport – American Eagle served DFW nonstop
Wikipedia: Fayetteville Regional Airport – United Airlines ceased United Express service March 7 2019
Wikipedia: Fayetteville Regional Airport – US Airways Express seasonal PHL service lost mid-2000s
Wikipedia: Fayetteville Regional Airport – US Airways Express served DCA nonstop

detail: Fayetteville lost United Express (IAD) on March 7, 2019 when United declared the route 'no longer sustainable'; today only American Airlines (CLT) and Delta (ATL) remain, down from five or more hub connections in the 1990s.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Florence Regional Airport – Eastern served FLO Atlanta route 1948–1965
Wikipedia: Florence Regional Airport – Piedmont Airlines Boeing 737 service ended 1981
Wikipedia: Florence Regional Airport – US Airways Express served multiple hubs through the 1990s

detail: Florence lost Piedmont Airlines mainline service (Boeing 737s) in 1981 and by the early 2000s had consolidated to only Charlotte connections; today served solely by American Airlines three daily CLT departures.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Anderson Regional Airport – CCAir USAir Express commuter flights to Charlotte 1992–1993
Wikipedia: Anderson Regional Airport – Southern Airways served ATL-Anderson-CLT route 1964–1974
Wikipedia: Anderson Regional Airport – Bankair commuter service to Columbia and GSP 1978–1984

detail: CCAir operating as US Airways Express provided the last scheduled service in 1992–1993 with commuter flights to Charlotte; Anderson has had no airline service since 1993.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Hilton Head Airport – Delta Connection (Mesaba) Saab 340 ATL service Mar 4 – Nov 1 2010
Wikipedia: Hilton Head Airport – United began seasonal ORD service spring 2019, suspended after COVID 2020
Wikipedia: Hilton Head Airport – United began seasonal EWR service spring 2019, suspended after COVID 2020

detail: Hilton Head saw its first jet service only in 2018 (Republic E175) but lost United's seasonal Chicago, Newark, and Dulles routes after COVID-19; currently served by American Airlines (CLT) and Delta (ATL) year-round.
